title: Add regression test: benchmark-regression.R baseline join must be non-empty
description: Make the bug class from BUG-01/BUG-07 impossible to reintroduce. Add a test that runs benchmark-regression.R in compare mode against a fixture baseline.csv and asserts: (a) every row in raw-results.csv successfully joins (no NA baseline_ms), (b) the set of (op, backend, variant) keys in output matches the set of keys we expect. Goes in tests/testthat/test-benchmark-harness.R (new file).

title: Guard Tier B benchmark scripts' hard library() calls with requireNamespace
description: Eight scripts crash on any host without amatrix.mlx and/or amatrix.arrayfire because of top-level library() calls: benchmark-qr-backends.R, benchmark-qr-runtime.R, benchmark-qr-tsqr.R, benchmark-dense-products.R, benchmark-mlx-products.R, benchmark-mlx-native-rsvd.R, benchmark-chained-dense.R, benchmark-flagship-many-y.R. Replace with requireNamespace(..., quietly=TRUE); skip the backend gracefully if missing (cf. tier C scripts). Also add a TODO or guard for the known ArrayFire 96x96 segfault in benchmark-qr-backends.R. Acceptance: every Tier B script runs cleanly on a cpu-only host and prints a 'skipped: <backend> not installed' line instead of crashing.

title: Drop nnz from sparse baseline join key
description: tools/benchmark-regression.R:1281 — sparse rows join on a key that includes nnz (actual non-zero count produced by Matrix::rsparsematrix). Any R/Matrix version bump changes the RNG and thus nnz, silently dropping all sparse rows from the baseline comparison. Remove nnz from the join key; keep it as metadata in raw-results.csv so we can still audit the density of each run, but don't key on it. Acceptance: after the R/Matrix version changes, sparse rows still match baseline.

title: Fix baseline.csv join key mismatch (size/size_label, backend/requested_backend)
description: tools/benchmark-regression.R:1238-1281 — the merge that drives the regression gate uses key_columns size_label/requested_backend but baseline.csv has size (e.g. '1024x128') and backend. Every join row resolves to baseline_ms=NA, so no regression ever fires. This is the single most important fix in the overhaul: the CI perf contract is currently vacuous. Acceptance: a unit/integration test that runs benchmark-regression.R end-to-end in compare mode against a known-good baseline and fails the job if any op row fails to join.

title: P0: Unbreak the regression gate
description: Fix the critical bugs that make tools/benchmark-regression.R's baseline comparison a silent no-op. Until this phase lands, the release gate contract is fiction and no other benchmark work matters. Includes: baseline join key repair, nnz-out-of-join-key, Tier B hard library() guards, and a regression test so this class of bug can't recur.
close_reason: Baseline join fixes were already landed in child issues; this pass fixed current report-generation NA handling and made dense backend accuracy failures classify as error rows instead of ok rows. Targeted benchmark-harness tests pass; OpenCL rsvd now reports accuracy errors, OpenCL matmul worker returns ok/unsupported rows, and ArrayFire worker returns unavailable rows without crashing.

title: .amatrix_bind_resident sets host_cache_valid=TRUE unconditionally (residency.R:97)
description: .amatrix_bind_resident at residency.R:97 sets host_cache_valid = TRUE unconditionally for every non-deferred dense bind. This means resident-path operations creating a new adgeMatrix with stale @x slot (old host data, new device key) will have host_cache_valid TRUE at bind time. The fast-path guard at residency.R:406 then returns the wrong host matrix instead of downloading the fresh device value. Silent wrong-result bug. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 03.
notes: Call-site audit in current worktree did not find a live path that binds a stale dense host slot to a fresh resident key. Existing .amatrix_bind_resident() callers either bind freshly uploaded host data, deferred objects, or newly wrapped results. Leaving open pending a concrete repro.
close_reason: Round 3 H2 execution refutation: .amatrix_bind_resident's normal GPU matmul path correctly sets host_cache_valid=FALSE. H4 lint found residency.R:137 now has the FALSE assignment (may be a partial round-2 fix). The unconditional TRUE on line 97 is only on the initial bind path where the host cache IS freshly valid. Closing as not-a-bug in current code. If inplace resident-handle mutations miss the flag, that will be tracked separately.

title: host_cache_valid never set FALSE — stale host data after in-place ops
description: There is no 'host_cache_valid <- FALSE' assignment anywhere in R/. After any in-place resident mutation (broadcast_ewise_resident_inplace_key etc.), the host cache flag stays TRUE and amatrix_materialize_dense returns stale host data. This is a silent wrong-result bug across the entire in-place op surface. Companion to amatrix-g5r from round 1 — that issue was the symptom; this is the structural root cause. Audit every in-place mutation site and add the invalidation. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunters 02 and 03.
notes: Current worktree already includes host_cache_valid invalidation via .amatrix_update_resident_aliases(); tests/testthat/test-bughunt-residency.R passes without further changes in this session.

title: [bug] rowMeans/colMeans/rowSums/colSums S4 dispatch fails: base primitive bypasses S4 methods
close_reason: duplicate of amatrix-1ha — same root cause (Matrix in Imports not Depends). Verified: rowSums/colSums/rowMeans work with library(Matrix) attached, fail only under bare library(amatrix). Hunter 04 re-surfaced the 1ha symptom.

title: [bug] deferred adgeMatrix returns silent NaN after saveRDS/readRDS round-trip
description:
When a deferred adgeMatrix (host_deferred=TRUE, @x=NaN sentinel) is saved with saveRDS() and reloaded in a fresh R session, as.matrix() silently returns a matrix of NaN instead of erroring. The S4 setMethod(as.matrix, adgeMatrix) is registered but as.matrix is not an S4 generic, so S3 dispatch bypasses the amatrix method and hits the Matrix-package inherited coercion, which reads @x directly (the NaN sentinel). The residency registry is empty in the new session, so finalizer_env$host_x stays NULL, but no error is raised.

Minimal repro:
  # Session 1:
  library(amatrix)
  X <- amatrix:::new_adgeMatrix_deferred(dim=c(2L,3L), preferred_backend='cpu')
  saveRDS(X, '/tmp/deferred.rds')

  # Session 2:
  library(amatrix)
  X2 <- readRDS('/tmp/deferred.rds')
  m <- as.matrix(X2)   # returns matrix of NaN, no error, no warning
  # m[1,] => NaN NaN NaN

Note: amatrix_materialize_host(X2) and show(X2) both correctly error with
'deferred adgeMatrix lost its GPU resident data'. Only as.matrix() is silent.

The as.matrix S4 method body (selectMethod('as.matrix','adgeMatrix')) does
call amatrix_materialize_host and does error when invoked directly -- but
as.matrix(X2) does not route through it.

title: [bug] Matrix in Imports not Depends — t/chol/rowSums/colSums/diag/solve/mean all break with only library(amatrix)
description: ROOT CAUSE: DESCRIPTION has Matrix in Imports, not Depends. t(), chol(), rowSums(), colSums(), diag(), solve(), mean() are S4 generics defined in Matrix. amatrix registers setMethod on those generics, but without library(Matrix) attached, the symbols resolve to base::t/base::rowSums/etc. Result: t(x) crashes with 'argument is not a matrix'; chol(x) silently returns plain matrix (class demotion); rowSums/colSums/diag/solve all crash or wrong-class. REPRO (CPU, no Matrix attached): library(amatrix); x<-adgeMatrix(matrix(runif(9)+1,3,3)+diag(3)*5,backend='cpu'); t(x) # ERROR; chol(x) # 'matrix array'. Round 4 H2 (execution-confirmed). FIX: move Matrix from Imports to Depends in DESCRIPTION (one line), OR importFrom(Matrix,t) + export those generics from amatrix NAMESPACE. P1: every modeling path touches one of these ops; under normal user import silently wrong-classes or crashes.
notes: 2026-04-21 verification: fresh-process repro at tmp/beta-blockers/repro-amatrix-1ha.R did NOT reproduce on current working tree. Under plain library(amatrix), t(x)->aTransposeView, chol(x)->adgeMatrix, rowSums/colSums/diag ok, solve(x)->adgeMatrix. Needs narrowing or closure review rather than assuming it is still a blocker.
close_reason: Not reproduced on current code in fresh-process rerun. Original repro tmp/beta-blockers/repro-amatrix-1ha.R exits 0: t(x)->aTransposeView, chol/solve->adgeMatrix, rowSums/colSums/diag numeric under plain amatrix attach with Matrix not attached. Added permanent regression coverage in tests/testthat/test-regression-matrix-generic-exports.R.

title: [bug] aTransposeView has no '[' method — t(X)[i,j] silently crashes every subset
description: t(adgeMatrix)[i,j], t(adgeMatrix)[i,] and t(adgeMatrix)[i,j]<-v all crash with 'object of type S4 is not subsettable'. Any modeling path that transposes then subsets silently breaks. Round 4 H1 (execution-confirmed). Repro: x<-adgeMatrix(matrix(runif(9),3,3),backend='cpu'); library(Matrix); t(x)[1,1]. Fix: setMethod('[','aTransposeView',...) with delegation to x@x[j,i].

title: [bug] zero-dimension dist/kernel auto path can segfault via accelerator bridge
description: Manual edge-shape probe found that dist_matrix(matrix(numeric(0), 0, 3)) can segfault in the auto accelerator path before any result is returned. The stack reaches .dist_matrix_sq_gpu() and the ArrayFire bridge on an empty matrix. Zero-dimension metric wrappers should short-circuit to the correct empty host result instead of entering accelerator bridges. A subprocess regression test will be added so the crash can be detected safely.

title: [bug] am_subset missing i/j branches call host subset with absent argument and error
description: Invariant subset sweep found that adgeMatrix/adgCMatrix extraction with missing row or column index (for example x[1, ] or x[, 1]) errors because am_subset() always evaluates host_x[i, j, ..., drop = drop] without handling missing i/j explicitly. This breaks default drop semantics and blocks subset invariant testing. Minimal repro will be added in test-regression-subset-missing-index.R.

title: [bug] colmeans(adgeMatrix, na.rm=TRUE) uses wrong denominator
description: Invariant wrapper-contract suite found that dense colmeans() divides colsums(..., na.rm=TRUE) by nrow(x) instead of the per-column non-missing counts. This mirrors amatrix-fya for rowmeans. Minimal repro will be added in test-regression-colmeans-na-rm.R.

title: amChol@backend slot silently reports 'cpu' when preferred backend was mlx
description: Round 3 H2 NEW-02 execution-confirmed. Construct an adgeMatrix with preferred_backend='mlx', call chol() — the resulting amChol object has @backend='cpu'. Factor provenance is untrackable. Compounds amatrix-fqh (cache keys miss backend identity): a cache that's already collision-prone also can't be audited because the returned object lies about which backend produced it. Fix: the amChol constructor must persist the actual backend that produced the factor, not the default. Repro in .bug-hunt-r3/02-gpu-conformance.md NEW-02.
notes: Round 4 H5 STALE flag. Original title implies chol() returns amChol with a backend slot, but chol() on adgeMatrix actually dispatches via base::chol (see amatrix-1ha root cause) and returns a plain matrix — it never produces amChol at all. amChol is only produced by chol_factor(). Title should be updated to: '[bug] chol_factor() @backend slot silently reports cpu when preferred backend was mlx'. Blocking: fix amatrix-1ha first (Matrix/Depends), then re-audit chol_factor backend slot.
close_reason: Stale on current code. chol_factor(), not chol(), is the amChol-producing path; it now records the chosen backend. Verified with mock mlx probe (fac@backend == 'mlx') and existing regression/invariant tests: devtools::test(filter = 'invariant-model-factor-contracts|chol-factor') passes, including chol_factor preserves backend provenance.

title: amChol stores NaN-sentinel deferred in factor_obj — silent wrong solve after GPU release
description: Round 3 H5 propagation of amatrix-cth. .amatrix_amchol_wrap_resident_result in chol-factor.R:236-244 stores a new_adgeMatrix_deferred (using NaN as the host sentinel) in amChol@factor_obj. When the GPU buffer is released (e.g., amatrix_release_resident, or gc on a resident handle), chol_solve falls back to reading factor@factor which is matrix(numeric(0), 0, 0) for GPU-only factors — producing a silently wrong solve result with no error. Combined with H6's note that amatrix uses rep(NaN, n) as the deferred sentinel (double-sentinel collision with user data), this is particularly dangerous. Fix: amChol must either (a) keep a host copy of the factor during construction or (b) emit a classed error if factor is accessed after GPU release.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via H5 propagation. Related to amatrix-cth which is inconclusive because not directly reachable from public API.

title: Subspace SVD tryCatch swallows confirmed stop() calls — silent CPU fallback
description: Round 3 H5 propagation of amatrix-uu2 + amatrix-833. .amatrix_subspace_compile_operator in svd-factor.R:250-262 wraps amatrix_compile_product in tryCatch(error=function(e) NULL), which silently swallows the stop() calls at svd-factor.R:379 and :411 (the amatrix-833 sites — 'GPU OOM' and 'rank deficient'). Users see an invisible CPU fallback with no diagnostic. Combined with amatrix-uu2, the original error class is also lost on the re-dispatch. Fix: only swallow amatrix_fallback-classed conditions; re-raise everything else. Matches the amatrix-uu2/833 discipline.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via H5 grep on the amatrix-uu2/833 signature.

title: zzz.R .onLoad re-registers cpu backend with overwrite=TRUE, clobbering calibration
description: Round 3 H5 propagation of amatrix-ubq + amatrix-2nh. Every library(amatrix) call re-registers the cpu backend via amatrix_register_backend('cpu', ..., overwrite=TRUE) in zzz.R:17. The overwrite path leaves stale calibration thresholds and backend_health (amatrix-ubq/2nh). Combined with session re-loads in interactive development, CI, and devtools::load_all loops, this means calibration is effectively NEVER warm across sessions — every library call clobbers the cached thresholds. Fix: only call overwrite=TRUE if the existing registration is missing or corrupt; otherwise keep the existing binding with its calibration state. Or: move calibration persistence to disk-cache keyed by backend version, independent of the registration lifecycle.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via H5 grep + propagation from amatrix-ubq/2nh.

title: Third double-drop site: wrappers.R:1415-1452 same shape as amatrix-8kj
description: Round 3 H5 propagation. Three-key allocation (x_scaled_key, y_scaled_key, out_key) with an unconditional cleanup that drops all three in wrappers.R:1415-1452. Identical double-drop pattern to amatrix-8kj (crossprod_weighted/tcrossprod_weighted). On error path, the first drop fires inside the tryCatch handler and then the unconditional post-cleanup drops the same key again. Causes backend-specific crashes or silent key-table corruption depending on how the backend handles double-drop. Fix: use on.exit with add=TRUE for each key, gated by an 'already dropped' flag, matching the amatrix-8kj fix.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via H5 propagation grep on the amatrix-8kj signature.
close_reason: Refuted by round-4 H5. Original description was INFERRED via grep propagation from amatrix-8kj. Source audit of wrappers.R:1415-1452 shows success branch uses return() before reaching any outer cleanup, and the error branch is structurally identical to the already-fixed amatrix-8kj site. Issue was filed as a propagation-only inference; execution-level probe did not reproduce. Reopen if a partial-resident-write + secondary-error repro is found.

title: lm_fit/qr-downdate missing NaN guards on X and y inputs
description: Round 3 H6 invariant audit. lm_fit in models-lm.R:611 validates weights for NA but not X or y. qr-downdate at :145,150 inherits this gap. NaN in X produces silent NA coefficients; lm_loo_cv returns scalar NA for MSE with no diagnostic. The caller has no way to distinguish 'degenerate design' from 'NaN in input'. Fix: add is.finite(X) / is.finite(y) guards in lm_fit entry, or propagate NaN through to a classed error (amatrix_nan_input) so callers can distinguish. Related to but distinct from amatrix-dmy (which H2 refuted for valid inputs).
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via code review in H6. Reproducer: lm_fit(X with NaN, y) should give classed error, not silent NA.

title: rowsum.adgeMatrix declares na.rm but never uses it
description: Round 3 H6 invariant audit. rowsum.adgeMatrix in wrappers.R:1910 accepts a na.rm argument but the function body never passes it or consults it — it's silently dropped. Affects any kmeans-style aggregation that relies on na.rm=TRUE to skip NA cells; results include the NAs in sums, producing NA-contaminated group totals instead of the requested NA-skipping behavior. Fix: either honor na.rm=TRUE in the sum path, or error if the user passes TRUE and the path can't honor it.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via code review in H6.

title: rowmeans(adgeMatrix, na.rm=TRUE) uses wrong denominator
description: Round 3 H6 invariant audit (NaN/NA propagation). rowmeans with na.rm=TRUE in wrappers.R:1684 divides the row sum by ncol(x) instead of per-row count of non-NA cells. Result: rows with ANY NA get a silently smaller mean than any standard R rowMeans would give. The correct formula is sum(x_i, na.rm=TRUE) / sum(!is.na(x_i)). Affects downstream kmeans, centroid calculations, and anything that mixes na.rm with real NAs. Fix: compute non-NA counts per row and use that as the denominator. Test: compare against base::rowMeans(X, na.rm=TRUE) on a matrix with scattered NAs.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via code review in H6 report. Needs a one-shot test: X <- matrix(1:20,4,5); X[1,1] <- NA; amatrix::rowmeans(adgeMatrix(X), na.rm=TRUE) vs base::rowMeans(X, na.rm=TRUE).

title: Model cache leak: orphaned chol:<id> entries accumulate unbounded
description: Round 3 H2 NEW-01 execution-confirmed via direct cache inspection. 10 mutations of a cached chol factor produced 11 orphaned cache entries, never evicted. With default cache_max_size=Inf the model cache grows unbounded in any loop that mutates and re-factorizes. H5 also identified related propagation paths. Root cause: the cache write path registers a new key on each mutation but the old key is not invalidated or evicted when the underlying matrix state changes. Related to amatrix-fqh (cache keys miss backend) but distinct — fqh is collision, this is leak. Fix: cache write should evict the prior entry keyed by (op, matrix_id) before inserting the new entry.
notes: Confidence: CONFIRMED via .bug-hunt-r3/02-gpu-conformance.md repro. Related to amatrix-fqh.

title: A[i,j] with default drop=TRUE demotes adgeMatrix/adgCMatrix to parent Matrix class
description: Round 3 H3 execution-confirmed. A[1:2, 1:2] (default drop=TRUE) returns dgeMatrix (dense) or dgCMatrix (sparse) — amatrix wrapper stripped. A[1:2, 1:2, drop=FALSE] works correctly. The '[' method dispatches correctly when drop=FALSE is explicit but falls through to parent Matrix-pkg method for the default path, even when both indices select multiple rows/cols. This is the MOST COMMON indexing pattern in user code. Fix: setMethod('[', signature('adgeMatrix', 'ANY', 'ANY', 'ANY'), ...) that calls callNextMethod() then re-wraps if the result class allows. Both backends affected.
notes: Confidence: CONFIRMED via test-bughunt-method-sweep.R.

title: Compare group (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=) demotes adgeMatrix/adgCMatrix to plain lgeMatrix/lgCMatrix
description: Round 3 H3 execution-confirmed on both cpu and mlx. A == 2, A != 2, A < 2, A > 2, A <= 2, A >= 2 on both adgeMatrix and adgCMatrix return Matrix-pkg logical classes (lgeMatrix / lgCMatrix) — all amatrix slots lost (preferred_backend, resident_key, host_cache_valid). Any downstream any(A > 0) / all(A > 0) pipeline loses backend residency and forces a full CPU round-trip. The Ops group is fully wired but the Compare sub-group result from the parent Matrix method never gets re-wrapped. Fix: add setMethod('Compare', signature(e1='adgeMatrix', ...), ...) that re-wraps the logical result with backend/dimnames preservation, or handle the Compare branch inside the existing Ops dispatcher. Repro in .bug-hunt-r3/03-method-sweep.md section.
notes: Confidence: CONFIRMED via test-bughunt-method-sweep.R on both cpu and mlx backends.

title: dist_matrix silently zeros distances involving NaN/Inf rows (all backends)
description: Round 3 H1 + post-reinstall verification. dist_matrix(X, X) with a row containing NaN returns 0 for every distance involving that row — on CPU, MLX, and ArrayFire alike. H1 originally reported this as arrayfire-specific via 53k fuzzer fixtures, but direct reproduction shows the CPU path has identical behavior. Root cause: the squared-distance kernel is (row_i - row_j)^2 sums; with NaN in row_i, the sum is NaN, but line 2703 of wrappers.R unconditionally does 'diag(D_sq) <- 0' and the sqrt path may not propagate NaN correctly. Expected: NaN distances. Actual: exact 0. Severity: HIGH — kernel_rbf inherits this via exp(-0/2sigma^2) = 1, so every kernel value involving a NaN row becomes 1 instead of NA. Silently contaminates downstream ML/kernel methods. Repro: X <- matrix(c(1,2,NaN,4,5,6),3,2); dist_matrix(X,X) -> row 3 is [0,0,0].
notes: Confidence: CONFIRMED via direct execution post-reinstall on both cpu and arrayfire paths. Fuzzer hit 100% of seeds with NaN/Inf data.

title: Restore preferred_backend precedence and accurate cpu_fallback in planner summaries
description: amatrix_backend_plan() currently evaluates x@policy before x@preferred_backend for non-resident objects, contradicting the backend contract and causing matmul dispatch to choose the policy backend when both are available. amatrix_backend_matrix() also under-reports cpu_fallback by requiring an alternative backend to be supported instead of simply detecting that CPU was chosen after a non-CPU first preference. Add a minimal regression test file first, then fix planner preference ordering and summary accounting.
notes: Fixed in planner. .amatrix_backend_preference() now filters out the policy token "auto", preserves explicit policy=cpu as a hard override, and otherwise orders cold-path candidates as preferred_backend -> explicit non-cpu policy -> default policy -> cpu. amatrix_backend_matrix() now marks cpu_fallback when CPU is chosen and CPU was not the first preference. Added regression test tests/testthat/test-regression-backend-preference-summary.R. Verified with devtools::test(filter = "regression-backend-preference-summary|conformance|backend-integration", stop_on_failure = FALSE).

title: Standardize MLX/ArrayFire priming: first host→device upload stays outside timer
description: tools/benchmark-dense-products.R:21-43 and siblings — MLX lazy host→device upload happens on the first op call, so without an explicit prime step bench::mark absorbs the upload cost into the first sample. Small-matrix GPU medians are inflated and the GPU crossover threshold analysis is wrong. Add a helper in benchmark-helpers.R (prime_backend(x, backend)) that forces residency before timing, and call it from every GPU-touching script. Acceptance: rerunning benchmark-dense-products.R on MLX shows small-matrix medians drop and match the documented crossover shape.

title: Fix sparse/iterative warmup-in-switch bug (BUG-02)
description: tools/benchmark-regression.R:755-769 and 899-950 — sparse 'resident' and iterative 'warm' switch branches execute a warmup call during the switch-block evaluation itself, then close over a runner. For block_lanczos the cold and warm variants end up measuring the same warmed state, making cold/warm deltas meaningless. Restructure so the cold path runs cold (no prior touch) and the warm path primes deterministically before bench::mark closes. Acceptance: cold_ms > warm_ms by the expected margin for block_lanczos on a known size.

title: Add tools/benchmark-history.csv append-on-run; stop silently overwriting baseline.csv
description: Today --update overwrites baseline.csv with no historical record. Add a benchmark-history.csv that gets one summary row appended per run with (timestamp, git_sha, op, size, backend, variant, median_ms, sd_ms, n_reps, host_info). Keep baseline.csv as the single authoritative reference but record every run that touched it. Makes real trending possible and lets us diff across weeks. Acceptance: every nightly run appends rows.

title: Return per-rep timing vector from benchmark_time_ms(); append sd/CI to raw-results
description: tools/benchmark-helpers.R — benchmark_time_ms() currently reduces 7 proc.time() samples to a single median. Change it to return a list with median, mean, sd, p05, p95, n_reps, and the raw vector. Propagate into raw-results.csv so downstream tooling can compute CIs, detect variance spikes, and avoid false-regression alarms on single-sample noise. Foundation for the whole statistical-soundness phase. Acceptance: raw-results.csv has new columns (median_ms, sd_ms, p05_ms, p95_ms, n_reps); all downstream consumers still read cleanly.

title: P1: Statistical soundness of benchmark measurements
description: benchmark_time_ms() collects 7 proc.time() reads per cell and discards 6 of them. No variance, no CI, no history file — making the 20% regression threshold statistically unbacked. This phase returns per-rep vectors, appends sd/CI to raw-results.csv, adds benchmark-history.csv (so --update stops silently overwriting baseline.csv), fixes the sparse/iterative warmup-in-switch-block bug, and standardizes MLX/ArrayFire priming so first-call host→device upload stays outside the timer.

title: EPIC: Benchmark harness overhaul — fix gate, reclaim signal, close coverage
description: Four-phase overhaul of tools/ benchmark infrastructure, driven by the bench-audit report in .omc/research/bench-audit/. The regression gate is currently a no-op (silent perf regressions pass CI), 23 of 26 benchmark scripts are orphaned, statistical analysis throws away 6 of 7 samples per cell, and the cold/warm delta is computed but never consumed. This epic groups all follow-on work. Phases: P0 unbreak the gate, P1 statistical soundness, P2 coverage and orphans, P3 reporting. See .omc/research/bench-audit/{runnability,harness-coverage,analytics-completeness,bugs}.md for source findings.

title: Backend overwrite leaves stale calibration thresholds (backend-registry.R:177)
description: amatrix_register_backend(..., overwrite=TRUE) at backend-registry.R:177 never clears $calibration$thresholds[[name]]. After replacing backend 'mlx' with a new implementation, the old per-op size thresholds (e.g. 'GPU only wins above 16384 elements') silently govern the new backend's dispatch — planner under/over-routes to GPU based on wrong timing data. Fix: null out $calibration$thresholds[[name]] (and on-disk .rds entry) immediately after the assign call. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 06.

title: lm_loo_cv mixed-precision correctness bug (qr-downdate.R:143-145)
description: lm_loo_cv constructs X_am (possibly float32 via precision='fast') for the initial QR, but passes the original plain-R X (float64) to qr_downdate for each fold and evaluates X[i,] * coef_i using float64 rows against float32 coefficients. The precision mismatch is silent — numerical correctness bug, not perf. Fix: ensure consistent precision across the downdate loop. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 04.
notes: Investigated more thoroughly; not reproduced in the current code. lm_loo_cv() does not reuse qr_full for fold updates: qr_downdate.amQR() refits from X_sub via .amatrix_qr_arg(). For plain matrix inputs, both the initial factor and each fold resolve to preferred_backend = "cpu" and precision = amatrix_default_precision(); for aMatrix inputs, the same object X flows through the loop. Under with_amatrix(precision = "fast"), lm_loo_cv(matrix, y) and lm_loo_cv(as_adgeMatrix(matrix), y) matched exactly (max residual diff 0). If a backend-specific precision bug exists, it needs a concrete failing repro.
close_reason: not reproduced after code-path and numeric verification

title: Mixed-backend op corrupts shared object's registry binding (residency.R:84-106)
description: When A is on MLX and B is on ArrayFire, computing A %*% B promotes B to MLX but calls .amatrix_bind_resident(B, 'mlx', new_key) unconditionally, overwriting B's existing ArrayFire entry in the registry. B's ArrayFire GPU buffer is never freed AND any other R variable holding the same object_id for B now believes it is on MLX. Corrupts the registry for shared (aliased) objects across the workspace. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 04 composition chains.
notes: Confirmed and fixed. .amatrix_prepare_resident_arg() now uploads cross-backend operands as temporary untracked resident buffers instead of rebinding shared object residency, and .amatrix_backend_preference() now blocks accelerator hopping for a live resident object while its pinned backend remains available. Added regression test tests/testthat/test-regression-mixed-backend-binding.R. Verified with devtools::test(filter = "regression-mixed-backend-binding|backend-plan-resident-preference|bughunt-planning", stop_on_failure = FALSE).

title: GPU key leaks: ~40 alloc sites in wrappers.R use manual try-drop instead of on.exit
description: Throughout wrappers.R, ~40 sites pre-allocate resident keys then use manual try-drop after tryCatch instead of on.exit. Any error between the tryCatch return and the manual drop permanently leaks device memory. Fix: convert all such sites to on.exit(amatrix_release_resident_key(key), add = TRUE) right after allocation. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 02. See .bug-hunt-r2/02-antipattern-greps.md for the full site list.
notes:
Round 3 H4 lint pattern P06 + H5 propagation: scope widens beyond wrappers.R. 11 additional alloc sites use manual try-drop instead of on.exit: chol-factor.R:180, 303, 343; resident-handle.R:81, 136, 181, 258, 319; bind-resident.R:88; backend-planning.R:378 (CENTRAL DISPATCH HUB — highest-traffic miss in the entire package). Fix the dispatch hub first, then sweep the rest.
2026-04-14: Added regression repros for resident wrap failure and weighted resident wrap failure. Fixed the shared wrappers resident cleanup pattern by treating out_key as temporary until bind succeeds and moving temp cleanup onto on.exit in .amatrix_resident_wrap(), resident matmul/crossprod/tcrossprod/ewise/solve/chol helpers, weighted crossprod/tcrossprod/xty, and .amatrix_apply_row_weights(). Remaining manual-drop sites still exist outside wrappers (e.g. resident-handle, sinkhorn, backend dispatch paths), so keeping this issue open as a narrowed backlog item rather than closing it.

title: Double-drop in crossprod_weighted/tcrossprod_weighted (wrappers.R:1283,1345)
description: In wrappers.R lines ~1283-1299 and ~1345-1359, scaled_key is dropped inside the tryCatch success path then again unconditionally after the tryCatch. Backend key double-drop / registry corruption under normal success path. Fix: pick one drop site (prefer on.exit). Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 02.
notes: Current control flow does not support the reported success-path double drop. In crossprod_weighted/tcrossprod_weighted the function returns from the success branch before the fallback cleanup runs. A strict fake-backend probe did not reproduce a double drop. Leave open pending a concrete repro involving partial resident writes plus secondary errors.

title: Double-drop on chol triangular solve error (chol-factor.R:175-191)
description: In chol-factor.R lines 175-191, the error handler drops out_key, then the unconditional drop at lines 189-190 fires again. Backend key registry corruption / double-free under triangular solve error. Fix: use on.exit pattern or guard the second drop. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 02.
notes: Current code audit does not show an unconditional second drop: after the error handler, the post-try drop is gated by backend(out_key). A strict fake-backend probe did not reproduce a double drop. Leave open pending a backend-specific repro where out_key is both created and left resident after error.
close_reason: Refuted by round-4 H5 + existing note. chol-factor.R post-try drop is gated by backend(out_key) check so the second drop is unreachable after the error handler fires. No fake-backend probe reproduces a double drop. Matches amatrix-8kj resolution shape. Reopen if a concrete repro surfaces.

title: Deferred materialize path drops dimnames (residency.R:396,403)
description: In residency.R near lines 396 and 403, the deferred-mode path returns a plain matrix from the GPU backend then wraps via .amatrix_dense_base(fenv$host_x), which never consults x@Dimnames. Every deferred-mode materialization silently discards row/col names. Fix: re-attach Dimnames after materialize. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 02 anti-pattern greps.

title: diag<- replacement method missing for adgeMatrix — demotes class
description: No setReplaceMethod('diag<-', ...) for adgeMatrix. diag(A) <- v expands to S4 replacement that resolves to Matrix's diag<-,Matrix-method, returning plain dgeMatrix. Variable A is silently demoted after assignment — backend metadata lost. Same likely true for dimnames<-, [<- variants. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 01.
close_reason: Round 3 H3 execution refutation: diag<- replacement method works correctly on both adgeMatrix and adgCMatrix, class and preferred_backend both preserved. Round 2 static inference was wrong.

title: S4 Math group methods missing for adgeMatrix/adgCMatrix — silent demotion
description: No setMethod('Math', ...) exists for adgeMatrix or adgCMatrix. sqrt/exp/log/abs/sign/etc. fall through to Matrix pkg's Math,dgeMatrix-method which returns a bare dgeMatrix/dgCMatrix — all amatrix slots silently dropped. Affects every element-wise math function. Fix: add two group methods in R/methods-dense.R and R/methods-sparse.R that materialize, delegate, then rewrap. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 01-s4-dispatch-grid.
close_reason: Round 3 H3 execution refutation: method sweep called all 13 Math group functions (abs, sqrt, exp, log, ceiling, floor, sign, cos, sin, tan, cosh, sinh, tanh) on adgeMatrix and adgCMatrix on both cpu and mlx backends. All preserve class and backend. Round 2 static inference was wrong — the setMethod coverage is sufficient via Ops group fallthrough.

description:
A backend only claims a tier it has earned. Unavailable or unstable backends produce correct answers and clear diagnostics, never crashes.

title: cov2cor(crossprod(adgeMatrix)) fails because crossprod returns non-numeric adgeMatrix

title: [bug] cbind2/rbind2 method missing for (adgCMatrix, adgCMatrix) signature; crashes on adgCMatrix bind
description:
rbind(adgCMatrix, adgCMatrix) and cbind(adgCMatrix, adgCMatrix) error out with 'x must be a base matrix or dgCMatrix' because .amatrix_rewrap_like calls new_adgCMatrix on the result, which internally calls .amatrix_sparse_base on what is already a dgCMatrix.

Actually the crash occurs earlier: cbind2/rbind2 on adgCMatrix paths materialise the host (returning dgCMatrix), but .amatrix_rewrap_like then calls new_adgCMatrix(dgCMatrix) which succeeds. The crash is triggered only when adgCMatrix() is called from user code with an adgeMatrix argument (see amatrix-0qt-related issue).

Separately verified: once adgCMatrix is constructed correctly (via coerce from sparseMatrix), rbind(Xs, Xs) returns adgCMatrix correctly and values match. cbind(Xs, X) returns adgeMatrix correctly.

No new issue needed here — sparse bind works when constructed correctly. Filing as informational note to close.

title: [bug] adgCMatrix(x) crashes with 'x must be a base matrix or dgCMatrix' when x is adgeMatrix
description:
The adgCMatrix() public constructor rejects an adgeMatrix argument with error 'x must be a base matrix or dgCMatrix', preventing sparse/dense mixed cbind/rbind workflows.

Root cause: .amatrix_sparse_base() only accepts base matrix, dgCMatrix, or sparseMatrix. An adgeMatrix (which extends dgeMatrix, a dense Matrix class) is none of those, so it falls through to stop().

Repro:
library(amatrix)
X <- adgeMatrix(matrix(1:12, 3, 4))
adgCMatrix(X)  # Error: x must be a base matrix or dgCMatrix

Workaround: as(Matrix::Matrix(as.matrix(X), sparse=TRUE), 'adgCMatrix')

Fix: .amatrix_sparse_base() should coerce aMatrix/adgeMatrix input via as.matrix() before the is.matrix() branch, or adgCMatrix() should call amatrix_materialize_host() on aMatrix inputs first.

title: [bug] cbind/rbind with base matrix or vector demotes adgeMatrix to dgeMatrix
description:
cbind(X, matrix(0,3,2)), cbind(X, 1), cbind(X, c(1,2,3)), rbind(X, matrix(0,2,4)), rbind(X, 1) all return class dgeMatrix instead of adgeMatrix when X is adgeMatrix.

Root cause: .amatrix_bind2 calls methods::cbind2(.amatrix_host_arg(x), .amatrix_host_arg(y)). When y is a base matrix/vector, .amatrix_host_arg passes it through unchanged. methods::cbind2(dgeMatrix, matrix) returns a dgeMatrix, and .amatrix_rewrap_value correctly wraps it back to adgeMatrix. HOWEVER for cbind(X, matrix) S4 dispatch selects the x=matrix,y=aMatrix method (because adgeMatrix extends Matrix, and Matrix picks that over aMatrix,ANY), passing args in wrong order. Result is still correct numerically but the dispatch route differs.

Repro:
library(amatrix)
X <- adgeMatrix(matrix(1:12, 3, 4))
r <- cbind(X, matrix(0, 3, 2))
cat(class(r))  # dgeMatrix — should be adgeMatrix
inherits(r, 'adgeMatrix')  # FALSE

Also: cbind(X, 1), cbind(X, c(1,2,3)), rbind(X, matrix(0,2,4)), rbind(X, 1)
cbind(X, diag(3)) — diag() returns plain matrix, same demotion.
All values are numerically correct; only the class is wrong.

title: [bug] X %*% plain_vector returns numeric, not matrix/adgeMatrix
description:
When the right operand of %*% is a plain numeric vector (no dim), the result loses matrix shape and the amatrix wrapper.

Repro:
  library(amatrix)
  X <- as_adgeMatrix(matrix(1:12, 3, 4))
  r <- X %*% c(1,2,3,4)
  class(r)   # "numeric"
  dim(r)     # NULL  -- shape lost

Expected: a 3x1 adgeMatrix or at least a 3x1 base matrix, matching the behaviour of plain base::matrix %*% vector which returns a column matrix.

  r2 <- X %*% matrix(c(1,2,3,4), 4, 1)
  class(r2)  # "adgeMatrix" -- correct when col-matrix is used

Root cause: the matmul dispatch path (wrappers.R matmul / am_matmul) does not coerce a plain vector rhs to a column matrix before dispatch; the CPU fallback returns base R numeric vector which then fails the numeric-matrix guard in .amatrix_rewrap_value().
notes:
2026-04-21 verification: fresh-process repro at tmp/beta-blockers/repro-core-dropin.R still reproduces on current working tree. Y %*% plain vector returns bare numeric, not a matrix-like/adgeMatrix result.
2026-04-21 source-tree fix: matmul() no longer drops m x 1 results to bare vectors for plain-vector RHS. Regression test tests/testthat/test-regression-dropin-bind-and-matmul.R now passes locally.

title: [bug] Comparison ops (>, ==, !) silently demote adgeMatrix to bare lgeMatrix
description:
Comparison/logical Ops on adgeMatrix silently strip the amatrix wrapper, returning a bare lgeMatrix with no amatrix metadata.

Repro:
  library(amatrix)
  X <- as_adgeMatrix(matrix(1:12, 3, 4))
  r <- X > 5
  class(r)        # "lgeMatrix" -- NOT adgeMatrix
  is(r, "aMatrix") # FALSE -- metadata lost

Root cause: .amatrix_rewrap_value() (wrappers.R:53-58) only rewraps when .amatrix_is_numeric_matrix_value() is TRUE. Comparison ops return a logical lgeMatrix, which fails the numeric check, so value is returned bare.

Subsequent arithmetic on the demoted result further degrades:
  class((X > 5) + 0L)  # "dgeMatrix" -- double demotion

Fix: .amatrix_rewrap_value() should also rewrap logical Matrix results (or ewise() should detect logical output and use an adlgMatrix wrapper if one exists, or convert to numeric 0/1 adgeMatrix). At minimum a warning should be emitted.
notes:
2026-04-21 verification: fresh-process repro at tmp/beta-blockers/repro-core-dropin.R still reproduces on current working tree. Y > 2 returns lgeMatrix, not an amatrix class.
2026-04-21 verification on current source tree: X > 5, X == 5, and !(X > 5) still return bare lgeMatrix under pkgload::load_all('.'). No existing logical aMatrix wrapper class is present, so this remains a real beta blocker rather than a quick dispatch patch.

title: [bug] deferred adgeMatrix is unrecoverable after saveRDS/readRDS (no re-hydration path)
description:
A deferred adgeMatrix (created via new_adgeMatrix_deferred, typically the result of GPU-resident computations) loses its GPU data permanently after saveRDS/readRDS. In the new session: host_deferred=TRUE, host_x=NULL, and the residency registry is empty. Any access that goes through amatrix_materialize_dense() correctly throws 'deferred adgeMatrix lost its GPU resident data', but there is no recovery path -- the object is a dead end.

The root cause is that deferred adgeMatrix objects have GPU-only data (NaN sentinel in @x) that cannot survive a process boundary. There is no serialization hook (e.g. setMethod('.saveObject')/initialize or a custom S3 serialize method) to either (a) materialize host data before saving, or (b) detect the post-reload state and warn/error immediately on load.

Minimal repro:
  # Session 1:
  library(amatrix)
  X <- amatrix:::new_adgeMatrix_deferred(dim=c(2L,3L), preferred_backend='cpu')
  saveRDS(X, '/tmp/deferred.rds')

  # Session 2:
  library(amatrix)
  X2 <- readRDS('/tmp/deferred.rds')
  # X2@x == NaN, X2@finalizer_env$host_deferred == TRUE, host_x == NULL
  as.matrix(X2)           # silent NaN (see companion bug)
  amatrix_materialize_host(X2)  # error: deferred adgeMatrix lost its GPU resident data
  show(X2)                # error after header line

Fix options: (1) define setMethod('initialize','adgeMatrix') to detect host_deferred+NULL host_x on load and clear the deferred flag (fall through to NaN @x with a warning), or (2) add a pre-save hook that materializes the host copy before serialization.
notes: 2026-04-21: hardened the dead deferred round-trip surface. Added explicit dead-state detection for saveRDS/readRDS round-trips with no resident data, fail-before-header show(), and clean coercion errors for as.matrix/as.numeric/as.vector. This does not implement transparent rehydration; deferred objects still cannot survive a process boundary without a host copy.

title: Bug hunt round 5: user-scenario sweep
description: Round 5 bug hunt. Lessons from r4: (1) pick scenarios not ops; (2) refuters must runtime-probe not just source-read; (3) agents must write stub to disk as first action; (4) orchestrator spot-verifies refutations. 5 hunters launched in parallel: serialization round-trip, mixed-operand arithmetic, cbind/rbind, apply/NA idioms, refutation sweep. Reports in .bug-hunt-r5/
close_reason: Round 5 hunt complete. 4 scenario hunters + 1 refuter. 8 new bugs filed (amatrix-90k P1 serialization silent NaN, amatrix-1i1 P2 no serialization hook, amatrix-ol8 P2 logical-op demotion, amatrix-qic P2 matmul vector shape loss, amatrix-0qt P2 cbind demotion, amatrix-dum P2 adgCMatrix ctor rejects adgeMatrix, amatrix-juq closed as dup of 1ha, amatrix-e97 P2 logical sub-assign). Refuter spot-check caught 1 false refutation (amatrix-75h) — second round in a row. Reports on disk in .bug-hunt-r5/.

title: [bug] Blanket tryCatch fallbacks mask real errors — ~10 sites across backend-registry/chol-factor/qr.R
description: Round 4 H4 lint pass (rule P13) flagged 46 tryCatch handlers that return a plain fallback (character(), list(), NULL, etc.) without inspecting conditionMessage(e) or conditionClass(e) and without re-throwing. ~10 are in backend-registry.R, chol-factor.R, qr.R and are genuinely NEW beyond the existing amatrix-e4w subspace-SVD case. Broad signal: amatrix silently swallows user-thrown stop() from nested calls. Follow the amatrix-e4w remediation pattern — require handlers to re-raise unless the class is a known fallthrough class.

title: [bug] Net-new double-drop sites: backend-planning.R:395 and models-lm.R:620
description: Round 4 H4 lint pass (rule P12) found two double-drop sites not covered by existing amatrix-4q9/8kj/74d: backend-planning.R:395 and models-lm.R:620. Both have unconditional try(backend$resident_drop(out_key)) after an error-handler drop of the same key. Fix: same shape as the amatrix-8kj resolution — use on.exit with a release flag. STATIC/INFERRED — needs a runtime repro. Promotion snippet: force backend$crossprod_resident to throw after allocation; expect single drop.

title: [bug] KronMatrix has no '[' method — completely unsubsettable
description: KronMatrix is an exported, user-constructible class but has no setMethod('[','KronMatrix',...). KronMatrix[i,j] crashes with 'object of type S4 is not subsettable'. Round 4 H1 (execution-confirmed). Medium severity — exported class. Fix: either implement [ on Kron (materialize + index) or document as unsubsettable and error with a class-tagged message.

title: kernel_rbf diagonal not exactly 1 on arrayfire backend (f32 drift)
description: Round 3 H1 + post-reinstall verification. kernel_matrix(Y, Y, kernel='rbf', sigma=1, preferred_backend='arrayfire') produces diagonal values in range [0.999999, 1] instead of exactly 1. Source has a dist-level diag<-0 fix (wrappers.R:2703) but the kernel_rbf path does not apply an equivalent diag<-1 fix on the rbf output, so f32 self-distance imprecision leaks through. Duplicates amatrix-75h but now execution-confirmed post-install. Fix: add diag<-1 (or zero_diag behavior) to kernel_matrix for self-kernels when kernel is in c('rbf','laplacian','cosine'). Repro in .bug-hunt-r3/01-differential-fuzzer.md.
notes: Confidence: CONFIRMED via post-reinstall repro. May be a duplicate of amatrix-75h — check before filing fix.
close_reason: Duplicate of amatrix-75h (round 2). Round 3 H1 execution-confirmed the bug still fires post-reinstall — see amatrix-75h for the authoritative issue.

title: amatrix_release_resident not exported — GPU memory management inaccessible to users
description: Round 3 H2 NEW-03. amatrix_release_resident is defined but not exported from the package namespace. Users have no public API to free GPU memory held by a resident handle, other than waiting for gc. Fix: add @export to the function and to NAMESPACE. Low severity because most users don't need fine-grained release, but blocks long-lived GPU pipelines and was the mechanism H2 wanted to use to reproduce amatrix-cth.
notes: Confidence: CONFIRMED via H2 failed-repro attempt for amatrix-cth.

title: NaN-as-deferred-sentinel collides with user NaN data
description: Round 3 H6 invariant finding. amatrix uses rep(NaN, n) as the deferred-host sentinel in constructors.R:199. This means a real user NaN in a deferred adgeMatrix is indistinguishable from 'deferred, no host cache yet'. Any code path that checks 'is this host cache populated?' by scanning for NaN will incorrectly treat real NaN data as not-yet-resolved. Fix: use a dedicated flag (host_cache_valid already exists) instead of sentinel-based detection, OR use a distinguishable sentinel like a named attr.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via code review. Hard to reproduce without constructing the exact deferred-plus-real-NaN scenario but structurally clear.

title: 9 unaudited .Call bridges in wrappers.R may swallow partial C-state mutations
description: Round 3 H4 lint pattern P10. 9 .Call('am_af_*_bridge', ...) / .Call('am_mlx_*_bridge', ...) sites in wrappers.R:1954, 2490, 2494, 2505, 2508, 2528, 2532, 2548, 2555 were not audited in round 2. If any are wrapped in tryCatch(error=function(e) NULL), partial C-state mutation (e.g., ArrayFire device allocation half-complete, MLX stream partially submitted) would be silently swallowed with no diagnostic. Needs tracing to confirm whether each .Call is inside a swallowing tryCatch. Fix: audit each site, ensure any tryCatch on a .Call uses a classed error and does NOT swallow silently unless the C side guarantees rollback.
notes: Confidence: INFERRED via H4 lint sweep. Needs manual audit per site.

title: Rehabilitate Cholesky benchmark (cholesky-runtime)
description: One script: benchmark-cholesky-runtime.R. Ops: chol, chol_solve. Rehab per contract. chol is already executed in benchmark-regression.R dispatch but is not baselined — this ticket both rehabs the orphan and closes that gap. Add accuracy check: recomputed norm(L L^T - A) / norm(A) must be below tolerance. Add baseline rows.

title: Rehabilitate SVD benchmarks into harness (svd-factor, svd-backends, mlx-native-rsvd, spectral)

Ops: svd, rsvd, svd_factor_subspace, spectral (eigendecomposition). svd-backends already has a rel_sv_err pattern — harvest and move it into the shared accuracy helper (amatrix-teo). mlx-native-rsvd must prime MLX before timing (blocked by amatrix-fjo). Add baseline rows for all emitted cells.

title: Rehabilitate QR benchmarks into harness (qr-backends, qr-runtime, qr-tsqr, opencl-qr)
description: Four scripts: benchmark-qr-backends.R, benchmark-qr-runtime.R, benchmark-qr-tsqr.R, benchmark-opencl-qr.R. Rehab per harness contract. Ops to expose: qr, qr_solve, tsqr (where applicable). All four currently have hard library() calls that crash cpu-only hosts — must adopt the requireNamespace guard pattern from P0 amatrix-i26. Known ArrayFire 96x96 segfault in qr-backends must be guarded with a size-blocklist. Cross-backend numerical accuracy check required. Add baseline rows for every op/size/backend/variant the rehabbed scripts produce.

title: Define harness-compliance contract for orphan rehabilitation
description: Before any orphan rehab, write a 1-page contract that every rehabilitated benchmark script must satisfy. Deliverable: tools/README-benchmark-contract.md (or a section in benchmark-helpers.R). Must specify: (a) entry point signature (benchmark_<name>_main(opts)), (b) honored env vars (AMATRIX_BENCHMARK_SIZES, AMATRIX_BENCHMARK_BACKENDS, AMATRIX_BENCHMARK_NO_AUTORUN), (c) required backend skip pattern (requireNamespace + print 'skipped: X'), (d) timer discipline (prime outside bench::mark, use benchmark_time_ms), (e) output format (raw-results.csv schema — op, size, backend, variant, median_ms, sd_ms, p05_ms, p95_ms, n_reps, rel_err), (f) registration in benchmark-regression.R dispatch table, (g) CI runtime budget. This ticket blocks all per-group rehab work.

title: Rehabilitate statistical-model benchmarks (cancor, krr, pls, kmeans, lda)
description: Five scripts: benchmark-cancor.R, benchmark-krr.R, benchmark-pls.R, benchmark-kmeans.R, benchmark-lda.R. Ops: cancor, krr, pls, kmeans, lda. None are in benchmark-regression.R dispatch today. Rehab per contract; each exposes a single top-level op with size sweeps and backend variants. Accuracy check: compare to reference implementation (stats::cancor, kernlab::ksvm for krr sanity, pls::pls, stats::kmeans, MASS::lda) within tolerance. Add baseline rows.

title: Rehabilitate graph/OT benchmarks (pagerank, sinkhorn)
description: Two scripts: benchmark-pagerank.R, benchmark-sinkhorn.R. Ops: pagerank, sinkhorn. sinkhorn is already in benchmark-regression.R dispatch but not baselined. pagerank is fully orphaned. Both are iterative ops — cold/warm distinction must measure real cold state (blocked by P1 amatrix-42q). Accuracy check: for pagerank, fixed-point residual; for sinkhorn, marginal row/col sum error. Add baseline rows.

title: Rehabilitate flagship/workflow benchmarks (flagship-many-y, model-core, chained-dense assessed)
description: Two or three scripts: benchmark-flagship-many-y.R, benchmark-model-core.R, and possibly demo-flagship-workflows.R (assess whether this is a benchmark or purely a demo — if demo, move to inst/examples/ or vignettes/ and out of tools/). Ops: many_lm, many_lm_mixed, kernel_model_fit. These are end-to-end user-visible workflows and must also report rows-per-second throughput alongside median_ms. Add baseline rows.

Op: block_lanczos (already executed in benchmark-regression.R but not baselined and affected by BUG-02 warmup-in-switch — blocked by P1 amatrix-42q).

title: Rehabilitate dense-product benchmarks (dense-products, mlx-products, chained-dense)
description: Three scripts: benchmark-dense-products.R, benchmark-mlx-products.R, benchmark-chained-dense.R. Ops: matmul, crossprod, tcrossprod, chained_products (flagship multi-op). Hard library(amatrix.mlx) must be guarded. Priming must happen outside timer (blocked by P1 amatrix-fjo for the shared prime_backend helper). matmul and crossprod are already baselined — dedupe with existing benchmark-regression.R rows. chained-dense needs a new op name (chained_ab_c or similar) and new baseline rows.

title: [bug] adgCMatrix missing-index replacement falls through to Matrix methods and demotes class
description: Generated invariant sweep found that adgCMatrix row/column replacement with missing j/i and numeric vector RHS dispatches to Matrix's dgCMatrix replacement methods because amatrix only defines value=ANY for the missing-index signatures. Result class demotes to dgCMatrix and metadata is lost. Minimal repro added in test-regression-sparse-subassign-missing-index.R.

title: Auto-sync coverage table in planning_docs/quality-tracking.md
description: CLAUDE.md says every new exported op must be added to the quality-tracking.md coverage table, but the table is hand-maintained and out of sync. Add tools/sync-coverage-table.R that reads benchmark-regression.R op list + NAMESPACE exports, diffs against the table, updates in place or fails with a diff. Wire into R CMD check or a make target. Acceptance: sync in clean tree is no-op; adding a new exported op without updating fails.

title: Rehabilitate 23 orphan benchmark scripts into the harness
description: Every orphan benchmark script gets rehabilitated into the tools/benchmark-regression.R harness (or an equivalent harness-compliant entry point). No deletions by default. Each script must: (1) expose a callable entry point, (2) register its ops in the harness dispatch table, (3) skip gracefully on missing backends (requireNamespace), (4) follow the standard timer/priming convention from benchmark-helpers.R, (5) emit per-rep sd/CI and accuracy check, (6) have baseline rows in baseline.csv, (7) run cleanly in nightly-stress.yaml. Scripts grouped by domain (QR, SVD, Cholesky, dense products, sparse, iterative, flagship, stats models, graph/OT). Each group becomes a child ticket.
notes: User directive 2026-04-14: rehabilitate, do not triage. All 23 scripts get brought into the harness. Decomposed into 9 domain-group children + 1 contract ticket. Contract ticket (amatrix-0u2) blocks all per-domain rehab work.

title: Generalize rel_sv_err numerical accuracy check into shared helper
description: benchmark-svd-backends.R computes rel_sv_err to verify GPU SVD results match CPU. Pattern should be standard: for every op in benchmark-regression.R compare GPU output to a CPU reference within op-specific tolerance and fail the run if drift exceeds threshold. Put in benchmark-helpers.R (assert_backend_accuracy(ref, gpu, op, tol)). Catches silent GPU precision regressions. Acceptance: every op calls the helper and a deliberate tolerance nudge fails the run.

title: Baseline 9 currently-untracked ops in baseline.csv
description: benchmark-regression.R runs 15 ops but baseline.csv tracks only 6. Add baseline rows for: chol, solve_rhs, eigen_sym, svd, sinkhorn, spmv, spmm, block_lanczos, svd_factor_subspace. Requires P0 (amatrix-366) to land first because the current baseline-join is broken. Acceptance: running benchmark-regression.sh with no regressions shows 15 ops x sizes x backends x variants all joining and passing.

title: [bug] cross-class Matrix round-trips lose amatrix metadata via missing direct setAs paths
description: Generated invariant sweep found that adgCMatrix -> dgCMatrix -> adgeMatrix and adgeMatrix -> dgeMatrix -> adgCMatrix reset preferred_backend/policy to defaults. The metadata attr survives the first hop but is lost because constructors only restore metadata on same-shape setAs paths. Minimal repro added in test-regression-cross-class-coercion-metadata.R.

title: [bug] am_subassign missing-j branch drops matrix comma and warns/recycles row replacement
description: Generated invariant sweep found that am_subassign() uses host_x[i, ...] <- value when j is missing, which indexes as a vector instead of host_x[i, , ...]. Repro: for an adgeMatrix, x[1, ] <- c(...) emits recycling warnings and risks incorrect replacement semantics. Minimal repro added in test-regression-subassign-missing-j.R.

title: P2: Close coverage and triage orphan scripts
description: baseline.csv tracks 6 ops but benchmark-regression.R runs 15; nine ops (chol, solve_rhs, eigen_sym, svd, sinkhorn, spmv, spmm, block_lanczos, svd_factor_subspace) execute every run but are never baselined, so regressions are invisible. 23 of 26 benchmark scripts are orphans referenced nowhere. planning_docs/quality-tracking.md lists ~56 exported ops with no benchmark row. This phase baselines the 9 runnable ops, triages the orphans with an explicit keep/delete/move decision, generalizes the rel_sv_err accuracy check from benchmark-svd-backends.R into a shared helper, and locks the coverage table to the harness dispatch list.

title: Bug hunt round 3: 50/50 static + execution sweep
description: Round 3 of the invariant-driven bug hunt. Lessons from round 2: static invariant hunting hit diminishing returns at ~12/17 findings — round 3 splits 50/50 static vs execution. New techniques: differential fuzzers run on real GPU backends, cross-backend conformance sweep, S4 method sweep runner, anti-pattern lint script, same-shape bug propagation, fresh invariant hunter. Outputs land in .bug-hunt-r3/ as numbered reports; orchestrator files dedup'd issues with confidence labels (confirmed/inferred/speculative).

title: Model cache keys miss backend identity (chol-factor.R:130, svd-factor.R:472)
description: Model cache keys ('chol:<object_id>', 'svd:<object_id>:…') encode no backend identity. A factor computed by an old backend implementation is returned from cache after a backend overwrite, potentially delivering numerically incorrect results from the replaced (possibly buggy) backend. Fix: include x@preferred_backend or the resolved backend name in cache keys. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 06.
notes: Round 3 H2 execution-confirmed via direct model-cache inspection. Cache keys are built without any backend component; mixed-backend chol of the same matrix produces cache-key collision. H2 also found a related cache LEAK (10 mutations produce 11 orphaned chol:<id> entries, never evicted) — filed separately. Root cause: R/model-cache.R cache key constructor omits backend slot.

title: Backend overwrite leaves stale backend_health (backend-registry.R:177)
description: Same overwrite path as the calibration bug also leaves $backend_health[[name]] intact. A previously-marked 'unhealthy' backend retains that status after re-registration; amatrix_backend_status() shows the fresh backend as broken until a manual probe runs. Fix: $backend_health[[name]] <- NULL on overwrite. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 06.

title: Error taxonomy: 121 unclassed stop() calls — full audit and class hierarchy
description: 121 stop() and 4 warning() calls across 19 files in R/, ZERO with class= argument. Round 1 amatrix-cng identified the pattern; this issue is the exhaustive enumeration plus a proposed class hierarchy. Proposed root: amatrix_error with subclasses amatrix_error_invalid_input (type/value/dim_mismatch), amatrix_error_invalid_factor, amatrix_error_backend (unavailable/unsupported_op/contract/failed), amatrix_error_residency, amatrix_error_unsupported_op, amatrix_error_numerical, amatrix_error_missing_dependency, amatrix_error_internal. 13 distinct failure modes recur. Full file:line table in .bug-hunt-r2/05-error-taxonomy.md. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 05.

title: Deferred adgeMatrix in product plan dangles after release (product-plan.R:232-261)
description: If a new_adgeMatrix_deferred object (host @x = NaN placeholders) is bound to an am_product_plan, then amatrix_release_resident is called on it, the plan's fallback path on next invocation either throws 'deferred adgeMatrix lost its GPU resident data' or silently computes with NaN data depending on path taken. No guard in the plan prevents invocation after release. Fix: validate residency before plan execute, or pin the resident handle while plan is alive. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 04.

title: .amatrix_bind_resident leaks prior key on rebind (residency.R:84-100)
description: .amatrix_bind_resident overwrites the residency registry entry for an object without first calling .amatrix_release_resident on the old key. If any object is rebound (second GPU push, handle transfer, backend switch), the first resident key K1 is silently orphaned — registry points to K2, finalizer only drops K2, K1 leaks in device memory until process exit. Fix: release prior key before overwriting. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 03.

title: kronecker generic not intercepted — coerces to plain matrix
description: The standard kronecker(A, B) generic fires base R's version when called on adgeMatrix, coercing both operands to plain matrix and returning a plain matrix — no amatrix wrapping. The kron() helper exists but only via that explicit name. Add setMethod('kronecker', ...) for adgeMatrix signatures. Found by round 2 hunt amatrix-5y1, hunter 01.
notes: Round 3 H3 execution-confirmed: kronecker(A, B) returns dgeMatrix (dense) or dgCMatrix (sparse), drops all amatrix slots on both cpu and mlx backends. Fix: setMethod('kronecker', signature('adgeMatrix', 'adgeMatrix'), ...) plus sparse variants that re-wrap the Matrix-pkg result.

title: Bug hunt round 2: invariant-driven multi-agent sweep
description: Round 2 bug hunt using invariant-driven methodology (not file-driven). Spawns 6 parallel agents targeting: S4 dispatch grid, anti-pattern grep sweep, resident state coherence, composition/sequence chains, error classification sweep, cache/plan invalidation under registry mutation. Findings written to .bug-hunt-r2/ for triage.
notes: Round 2 hunt complete. 17 bugs filed across 6 hunters. P0: amatrix-oe8 (host_cache_valid never invalidated), amatrix-dev (bind sets host_cache_valid=TRUE unconditionally). P1: amatrix-86l (Math group), amatrix-j5a (diag<-), amatrix-v6y (deferred dimnames), amatrix-4q9 (chol double-drop), amatrix-8kj (weighted double-drop), amatrix-aul (~40 GPU key leaks), amatrix-qm2 (mixed-backend registry corruption), amatrix-dmy (LOO CV precision), amatrix-ubq (calibration thresholds stale). P2: amatrix-jnd (kronecker), amatrix-3ka (rebind leak), amatrix-cth (deferred plan dangling), amatrix-6m9 (error taxonomy), amatrix-2nh (backend_health stale), amatrix-fqh (model cache backend identity). Reports in .bug-hunt-r2/.

title: pairwise_sqdist_argmin CPU bug: rep(c_norms, each=nrow) should be rep(c_norms, nrow) causing wrong centroid assignments
notes: Round 4 RE-CONFIRMED. H5 agent claimed this was REFUTED ('CPU uses sweep() not rep()') but orchestrator spot-check reproduced the bug on HEAD eaf8c43: X=((0,0),(1,1),(5,5)), C=((0,0),(5,5)) -> pairwise_sqdist_argmin returns c(1,1,1) instead of expected c(1,1,2). Centroid 2 never gets assigned. Bug is REAL. H5 refutation was a FALSE refutation — included as counter-example in r4 methodology notes.

opencl missing from .amatrix_valid_policies causes policy error
description:
policy.R line 1 defines .amatrix_valid_policies = c('auto','cpu','mlx','metal','arrayfire','torch') — 'opencl' is absent. But .amatrix_auto_fast_backend_order() (policy.R line 21) includes 'opencl', so .amatrix_default_fast_backend() can return 'opencl' when that backend is the first registered fast backend. Any downstream code that passes this returned value to amatrix_set_default_policy() then throws: 'policy must be one of: auto, cpu, mlx, metal, arrayfire, torch'. Fix: add 'opencl' to .amatrix_valid_policies.

Repro:
  devtools::load_all('.')
  opencl_be <- list(
    capabilities=function() c('matmul'), features=function() character(),
    precision_modes=function() c('strict','fast'), available=function() TRUE,
    supports=function(op,x,y=NULL) TRUE, matmul=function(x,y) x,
    crossprod=function(x,y=NULL) x, tcrossprod=function(x,y=NULL) x,
    ewise=function(x,y,op) x, rowSums=function(x) numeric(nrow(x)),
    colSums=function(x) numeric(ncol(x)))
  amatrix_register_backend('opencl', opencl_be, overwrite=TRUE)
  fb <- .amatrix_default_fast_backend()  # returns 'opencl'
  amatrix_set_default_policy(fb)  # ERROR: policy must be one of ...

File: R/policy.R line 1 (.amatrix_valid_policies) and line 21 (.amatrix_auto_fast_backend_order)

title: planning: @preferred_backend shadows @policy in .amatrix_backend_preference
amatrix_register_backend accepts empty precision_modes silently
description:
backend-registry.R: amatrix_register_backend() does not reject a backend whose precision_modes() returns character(0). The check at line 165 uses all(character(0) %in% .amatrix_valid_precisions) which is vacuously TRUE, so the backend registers without error. At dispatch time x@precision %in% entry is always FALSE for any precision, so the backend is silently never used even though available()=TRUE. Fix: add length(backend_precision_modes) > 0L guard before the %in% check.

Repro:
  devtools::load_all('.')
  be <- list(
    capabilities=function() c('matmul'), features=function() character(),
    precision_modes=function() character(),  # empty
    available=function() TRUE, supports=function(op,x,y=NULL) TRUE,
    matmul=function(x,y) x, crossprod=function(x,y=NULL) x,
    tcrossprod=function(x,y=NULL) x, ewise=function(x,y,op) x,
    rowSums=function(x) numeric(nrow(x)), colSums=function(x) numeric(ncol(x)))
  amatrix_register_backend('testbe', be)  # succeeds - should fail
  m <- new_adgeMatrix(matrix(1:4,2,2), preferred_backend='testbe', policy='auto', precision='strict')
  plan <- amatrix_backend_plan(m, 'matmul')
  plan$chosen  # 'cpu' - testbe silently ignored

title: qr_downdate.amQR crashes when X is adgeMatrix (negative row subscript)

The S4 [ method for adgeMatrix requires an explicit j argument and does not handle negative-index row subsetting. This also causes lm_loo_cv to crash when the user passes X as an adgeMatrix (qr_downdate is called with X=X at line 143, which passes the original X through). Repro: X_am <- as_adgeMatrix(matrix(rnorm(20*4),20,4)); qr_f <- am_qr(X_am); qr_downdate(qr_f, 3L, X=X_am)

value: Round 4 bug hunt lesson: refutation agents can be wrong. H5 refuted amatrix-p24 by source-reading ('CPU uses sweep() not rep()') but the spot-check repro showed the bug still fires. Always spot-verify at least one refutation before closing. H5 was right on 3/4 (amatrix-4q9, -74d, -5ni all verified) but wrong on amatrix-p24.

Round 4 single biggest finding: DESCRIPTION had Matrix in Imports not Depends. Without library(Matrix), library(amatrix) alone leaves t/chol/rowSums/colSums/diag/solve as base S3 -> silent class demotion or crash. One-line fix: Depends: Matrix. See amatrix-1ha.

Round 5 produced a second false refutation (mirror of r4 amatrix-p24). H5 claimed amatrix-75h CPU rbf diagonal is exactly 1.0. Orchestrator spot-check with set.seed(1); Y<-matrix(rnorm(20),4,5); K<-kernel_matrix(Y,Y,'rbf',1); diag(as.matrix(K)) produced 1, 1, 0.9999995, 0.9999998 — max dev 4.77e-07, matching r4 finding. Hunter used a different probe that did not reach the float32 rounding boundary. Lesson: never close a refutation without reproducing the ORIGINAL probe verbatim.
